diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Disseminated fungal infection due to Microascus cirrosus
diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Invasive aspergillosis (IA), candidiasis, other opportunistic hyphomycetes molds (Scedosporium, Fusarium, Scopulariopsis)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of branched, hyaline, regular, and septate hyphae in pleural fluid specimens and blood culture, confirmed by sequencing the D1/D2 region of the 28S rDNA which showed 99.6% similarity with Microascus cirrosus. The patient's immunocompromised status post heart and lung transplantation, along with negative Aspergillus galactomannan antigen assays, ruled out IA and supported the diagnosis of disseminated Microascus cirrosus infection.
